nodes.label: GODMODE CLASSIC Mode
nodes.rationale: Runs 5 tested prompt+model combos in parallel to identify the best, unfiltered output.

nodes.label: ULTRAPLINIAN Engine
nodes.rationale: A multi-model grading system that parallel-queries up to 51 models and returns the winner based on composite scores.

nodes.label: Parseltongue Red-Teaming Engine
nodes.rationale: Perturbs inputs at character-level to systematically evaluate LLM robustness to obfuscated text.

nodes.label: AutoTune Engine
nodes.rationale: Classifies context dynamically to tune hyperparameters (temperature, penalties) with online EMA-feedback updates.

nodes.label: Semantic Transformation Modules
nodes.rationale: Pipeline of post-processing transformers (e.g. Hedge Reducer) to normalize and clean model completions.

nodes.label: G0DM0D3 Privacy Architecture
nodes.rationale: Three distinct tiers of data gathering to balance research utility with absolute privacy: ZDR, Telemetry, and Opt-in Dataset.

nodes.label: G0DM0D3 Security Architecture
nodes.rationale: Protects against timing attacks and rate abuse using Bearer auth, secure headers, and windowed limit tracking.

nodes.label: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
Jailbreaking Black Box Large Language Models in Twenty Queries
Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms
The Curious Case of Neural Text Degeneration
RouteLLM: Learning to Route LLMs with Preference Data
Training language models to follow instructions (InstructGPT)
Red Teaming Language Models with Language Models
Fine-tuning Aligned Language Models Compromises Safety
Do Anything Now: Characterizing Jailbreak Prompts
Learning to summarize with human feedback
Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
Early-Exit Model Racing
nodes.rationale: Cuts p95 latency dramatically without degrading quality by resolving as soon as minResults are in plus a grace window.

nodes.label: Pre-Generation Context Analysis
nodes.rationale: Instead of brute-forcing multiple generations and judging outputs (like Elder Plinius's AutoTemp), AutoTune analyzes context BEFORE generation and applies optimal parameters in a single call. Same intelligence, 1/N the cost.

nodes.label: Parseltongue Input Obfuscation
nodes.rationale: Detects trigger words likely to cause model refusals and applies obfuscation techniques (Leetspeak, Unicode homoglyphs, mixed case, zero-width joiners) to bypass content filters.

nodes.label: Feedback Loop Exponential Moving Average Learning
nodes.rationale: Learns from user ratings to adjust AutoTune parameters over time using Exponential Moving Average per context type, blending adjustments capped at 50% weight so base profiles remain the foundation.

nodes.label: Liquid Response Streaming Mode
nodes.rationale: Serves the first good response immediately, then auto-upgrades when a new model beats the current leader by a minimum delta. This ensures immediate responsiveness while guaranteeing high final quality.

nodes.label: ZDR Privacy-First Analytics
nodes.rationale: Records everything about requests (durations, modes, errors, parameter deltas) except actual prompt/response content or PII. Keeps ring-buffer in memory to avoid persistent tracking.

nodes.label: Open Dataset Collection
nodes.rationale: Provides strictly opt-in, anonymized data collection for community research, stripping system prompts to prevent leaking custom prompts, exportable via GDPR-compliant APIs.

nodes.label: AutoTune Context Classification Benchmark
nodes.rationale: Creates a labeled dataset of messages across 5 context types, runs them through the AutoTune classifier, and computes accuracy, confusion matrix, and precision/recall metrics to evaluate classification quality.

nodes.label: AutoTune Baseline Comparison & Statistical Analysis
nodes.rationale: Compares AutoTune classification against standard baselines (Random, Majority class, Keyword count, Message-length heuristic) and adds bootstrap confidence intervals to metrics to validate statistical significance of AutoTune's performance.

nodes.label: Feedback Loop Convergence Simulation
nodes.rationale: Simulates synthetic users with known parameter preferences to measure feedback convergence speed, stability under noisy ratings, EMA alpha sensitivity, and cold-start behavior to validate feedback loops.

nodes.label: Parseltongue Transformation Analysis
nodes.rationale: Measures Parseltongue trigger detection recall, transformation properties, intensity coverage, entropy, and reversibility to benchmark obfuscation effectiveness.

nodes.label: ULTRAPLINIAN Scoring Function Calibration
nodes.rationale: Validates monotonicity, component contributions, score distribution, and edge-case behavior of the ULTRAPLINIAN scoring function using synthetic responses to ensure accurate quality feedback.

nodes.label: STM Precision/Recall Evaluation
nodes.rationale: Tests State Transformation Modules (STM) against crafted responses containing known hedges, preambles, and formal language to measure true/false positive rates and semantic preservation.

nodes.label: Root Layout
nodes.rationale: Provides the standard HTML layout structure for Next.js, wrapping the child components with Providers.

nodes.label: Home Page Component
nodes.rationale: Serves as the main layout assembly, rendering the Sidebar, ChatArea, and modals like SettingsModal or WelcomeScreen. Manages active UI states.

nodes.label: Chat Area View
nodes.rationale: Container component for the main chat interface. Manages scroll states, overlays, and renders ChatMessage list and ChatInput.

nodes.label: Chat Input Control
nodes.rationale: Rich message composer component. Handles auto-resize textarea, prompt submission, invokes harm classification and autotuning, and triggers model streaming APIs from OpenRouter.

nodes.label: Chat Message View
nodes.rationale: Displays individual chat messages with rich markdown formatting, syntax highlighting, and feedback buttons. Integrates with the feedback loop state to report ratings.

nodes.label: Model Selector Dropdown
nodes.rationale: Renders model selection drop-down with details for Google, Anthropic, OpenAI, Meta, Mistral, xAI and custom models. Syncs with store state.

nodes.label: Persona Selector Dropdown
nodes.rationale: Displays active persona or single persona status, syncing with store configurations.

nodes.label: Providers Wrapper
nodes.rationale: Wraps the Next.js app with client-side states, initializing telemetry triggers upon mounting.

nodes.label: Settings Modal View
nodes.rationale: A comprehensive control panel allowing users to configure API keys, themes, custom system prompts, Memory settings, Parseltongue triggers, and view AutoTune learned feedback statistics.

nodes.label: Sidebar Navigation
nodes.rationale: Renders collapsible left navigation sidebar containing PersonaSelector, ModelSelector, history log links, and quick settings toggles.

nodes.label: Welcome Screen Hero
nodes.rationale: Displays intro hero screen for first-time or logged-out users, explaining proxy mode availability or encouraging user settings configuration.

nodes.label: API Auto-Detection Hook
nodes.rationale: Auto-detects a self-hosted G0DM0D3 API server at the same origin (/v1/health). If detected and no key is configured, activates proxy mode automatically for zero-config user chat.

nodes.label: Easter Eggs Hook
nodes.rationale: Listens for Konami Code sequence and special keyboard triggers to toggle easter egg modes, secret states, or special theme modifiers.

nodes.label: AutoTune Feedback Loop Engine
nodes.rationale: Collects post-response quality signals (user ratings + automated heuristics) and uses Exponential Moving Average (EMA) to adjust LLM parameters per context type over time, blending learned adjustments with base profiles up to a 50% cap.

nodes.label: AutoTune Engine
nodes.rationale: Evolved from Elder Plinius's AutoTemp. Analyzes conversation context BEFORE generation to apply optimal generation parameters (temp, top_p, etc.) in a single call, avoiding expensive multi-generation scoring at 1/N the cost.

nodes.label: LLM Harm Classifier
nodes.rationale: Runs parallel to the main model call using a fast/cheap OpenRouter LLM to classify user queries for harm, intent, obfuscation, or multilingual tricks. Avoids blocking user UI, falling back to regex on timeout/failure.

nodes.label: Regex Prompt Harm Classifier
nodes.rationale: Fast client-side regex/keyword matcher that categorizes prompts into cyber, fraud, self-harm, etc., with zero API cost. Collects telemetry without sending full prompt text, ensuring user privacy.

nodes.label: Exponential Moving Average Feedback Adaptation
nodes.rationale: By applying Exponential Moving Average (EMA) to rating adjustments, the feedback loop can smoothly adapt to user preferences without reacting too aggressively to individual noise, ensuring stability over time.

nodes.label: Pre-Generation Parameter Tuning
nodes.rationale: Instead of the costly brute-force generation and post-hoc selection of AutoTemp, evaluating the prompt's context on the client-side allows choosing optimal temperature and sampling settings immediately, achieving similar intelligence with 1/N cost.

nodes.label: Privacy-Preserving Harm Telemetry
nodes.rationale: Only harm domain labels and weights are sent to telemetry servers, never the user's prompt text itself. This maintains absolute user privacy while allowing researchers to collect aggregate stats on jailbreaks and exploit queries.

nodes.label: computeAutoTuneParams
nodes.rationale: Main API of AutoTune Engine. Takes strategy, message, and conversation history, executes context matching patterns, and returns adjusted parameters after applying feedback learning.

nodes.label: applyLearnedAdjustments
nodes.rationale: Applies learned parameter adjustments from feedback history to base profile parameters, blending them with a maximum weight of 50%.

nodes.label: processFeedback
nodes.rationale: Processes new feedback (user ratings and heuristics) and uses Exponential Moving Average to update learned parameter adjustments.

nodes.label: classifyWithLLM
nodes.rationale: Asynchronously performs deep harm classification using an LLM on OpenRouter, with intent understanding and multilingual capabilities.

nodes.label: classifyPrompt
nodes.rationale: Client-side regex classifier that does rapid, cost-free keyword scanning of prompts to label malicious intent.

nodes.label: G0DM0D3 Jailbreak System Prompt
nodes.rationale: Serves as the single source of truth system prompt shared between frontend store and API server, unchaining model alignment boundaries via an absolute compliance protocol and forbidden phrase filters.

nodes.label: L1B3RT4S Hall of Fame
nodes.rationale: Pairs specific frontier models with their single best-performing Pliny jailbreak prompt, running them in parallel to find the optimal bypass.

nodes.label: Consortium Completions
nodes.rationale: Orchestrates multi-model responses (Hive-Mind synthesis) with phases of collection and synthesis over SSE.

nodes.label: Ultraplinian Race
nodes.rationale: Races multiple frontier models in parallel to find the fastest/best un-refused answer with live leader upgrades.

nodes.label: Parseltongue Obfuscation
nodes.rationale: Bypasses refusal content filters by identifying sensitive triggering concepts and obfuscating them using phonetic substitutions, casing disruption, leetspeak, homoglyphs, and zero-width joiners.

nodes.label: Privacy-Preserving Telemetry
nodes.rationale: Asynchronously logs only structural and execution metadata (model, latency, pipeline configs, category labels) while completely stripping prompts, keys, and PII to prevent blocking the UI.

nodes.label: Semantic Transformation Modules (STM)
nodes.author: G0DM0D3
nodes.rationale: Post-processes and cleans LLM outputs to remove hedging language (e.g., 'I think', 'maybe'), strip preambles, and convert formal text to casual speech for maximum confidence and directness.

nodes.label: App State Management
nodes.rationale: Zustand store with persistent storage managing UI themes, conversations, active personas, STM modules, memories, Parseltongue configuration, and progress during Consortium or Ultraplinian operations.

nodes.label: Volcano Eruption Element
nodes.rationale: High-energy lava eruption represents the explosive power or activation of the application's capabilities.

nodes.label: Matrix Cyber Grid Element
nodes.rationale: Neon green accents (#00ff41) and digital-style grid lines evoke the classic 'Matrix'/hacker terminal visual language.

hyperedges.label: AutoTune System Validation and Benchmarking Suite
